definition: Breaking a complex problem into meaningful subproblems or components.
Ordering actions decisions transformations checks branches loops and stopping conditions.
A smaller task within a larger problem.
A bounded unit with a clear responsibility.
A defined way for components to exchange inputs outputs or actions.
A relationship in which one component relies on another.
A procedure that solves a problem by reducing it to smaller versions of itself.
Reassembling parts into a coherent whole that still serves the original purpose.
Risk created by fragmentation hidden dependencies local optimization context loss or responsibility diffusion.
